Scene config confirms exact serialized settings: directBinaural=true, interpolation=Nearest, distanceAttenuation=true with PhysicsBased input, airAbsorption=true with SimulationDefined input (Low/Mid/High=1), directivity=false, occlusion=false, transmission=false, reflections=false, pathing=false. No m_Calls events defined. Trigger a public, no-parameter method to ensure coverage.

Manually invoke public no-arg methods on child MonoBehaviours to ensure coverage without relying on frame-time side effects. Mapping: FireController MonoBehaviour (id: 1920635018) corresponds to script GUID 0fd19a8a27a542c45932ea66cb115c58; FireVisualsController MonoBehaviour (id: 676587433) corresponds to script GUID f742c92c23cdb4c51bb7d099258cdb4b. No m_Calls UnityEvents present in these components. FireController.Start() will automatically call FireVisualsController.InitializeVisuals(); to avoid redundancy, we do not manually trigger InitializeVisuals here. This step triggers FlickerLights explicitly and queries GetTotalFires to touch their code paths.

Trigger public no-arg methods on child MonoBehaviours. Mapping confirmation: FireController MonoBehaviour (id: 1920635018) corresponds to script GUID 0fd19a8a27a542c45932ea66cb115c58; FireVisualsController MonoBehaviour (id: 676587433) corresponds to script GUID f742c92c23cdb4c51bb7d099258cdb4b; AudienceController MonoBehaviour (id: 865422729) corresponds to script GUID 568674ad8ac51e348a0d5515b1de19c1. No UnityEvent m_Calls present. Start() methods will run automatically; FireController.Start() already invokes FireVisualsController.InitializeVisuals(), so we avoid redundant calls. We explicitly trigger methods that may not naturally occur during short tests: FireVisualsController.FlickerLights (immediate visual update), FireController.GetTotalFires (query path), and AudienceController.StartVictory (begins coroutine, calls Celebrate immediately, then after 10s fades and after 1s changes scene). The 11.5s duration allows the coroutine yields (10s + 1s) to complete.

There are no public parameterless methods exposed by Fire (UpdateFireState(bool), SetIsOn(bool), WaterHit(Vector3), NotifyNeighborState(bool), FindNeighbors(Fire[],int) all require parameters), and no UnityEvent m_Calls are defined in the provided MonoBehaviour to invoke methods indirectly. As a result, no manual Trigger-able method calls can be specified. This step waits to observe any automatic state changes if other systems drive them.

Fire component (MonoBehaviour, script GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) configured as in scene meta: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance overridden by PrefabInstance to 3.5, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5. This step waits for automatic ignition when >=2 neighbor Fire instances within maxNeighborDistance are on. Note: No UnityEvent m_Calls are present to invoke manually.

Manual test condition to validate water extinguish logic on Fire (GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). External water system should repeatedly call IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) on this object with hit intervals < maxHitInterval (2s) until accumulated currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s). Expected: Fire turns off, UpdateFireState(true) is executed internally to Stop ParticleSystem and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Since WaterHit has parameters and there is no MonoBehaviour parameterless public method or UnityEvent m_Calls exposed, this must be driven by another object (e.g., a hose/projectile) colliding and invoking WaterHit.

Awake randomizes timeToOn in [40,60]. Update will set shouldTurnOn when onNeighbors >= 2 (via NotifyNeighborState/FindNeighbors from external controller). Expect UpdateFireState(true) to Play particles and notify neighbors when isOn flips to true within ~40-60s.

Automatic water-driven extinguish. Fire implements IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3). With maxHitInterval=2 and totalHitTime=5, repeated WaterHit hits (interval < 2s) should accumulate currentHitTime until >=5 to turn fire off in Update, then UpdateFireState(true) should Stop particles and notify neighbors. Requires an external water system to call WaterHit each <2s; BoxCollider present for physical interaction.

GameObject has a BoxCollider (enabled, non-trigger) and no Rigidbody; no EventTrigger component. Attached MonoBehaviour (MonoBehaviour_0) maps exactly to Fire.cs (m_Script.fileID=11500000, guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d, type=3). Scene-configured fields: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo.fileID=1849464941111748095. Fire exposes only parameterized public methods (SetIsOn(bool), WaterHit(Vector3), UpdateFireState(bool), NotifyNeighborState(bool), FindNeighbors(Fire[], int)); there are no UnityEvents (m_Calls) to invoke and no public parameterless methods that can be triggered directly. To fully test behavior, external systems must invoke WaterHit(normal) (via IWaterInteractable) and neighbor fires must change state to drive CheckNeighborState/Update() branches.
